growth_records.medium: L1+Si artificial seawater (ASW) with cocultured diatom Thalassiosira pseudonana CCMP1335 as sole organic-matter source
growth_records.cite: Schreier, Jeremy E. et al. A mutant fitness assay identifies bacterial interactions in a model ocean hot spot. Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America (PNAS) (2023). DOI:10.1073/pnas.2217200120. [PMC10041152]
growth_records.method: reported (mu reported as 0.95 d^-1 for first 4 d; converted to per-h and doubling time calculated)
growth_records.snippet: R. pomeroyi growth rate in the model systems (0.95 d-1 for the first 4 d of the study and 0.31 d-1 for the 8 d average) fell within the range reported for Rhodobacteraceae in natural marine systems
growth_records.notes: Experimental growth rate of the prokaryote R. pomeroyi DSS-3 in a diatom (T. pseudonana) coculture, measured by flow-cytometry cell counts (cells mL^-1) with CFU-based partitioning of R. pomeroyi within the community over an 8-d experiment. Paper reports two windows: mu1=0.95 d^-1 (first 4 d, exponential) and mu2=0.31 d^-1 (8-d average). Conversions: mu1=0.95/24=0.0396 h^-1; doubling time td=ln2/mu1=0.6931/0.0396=17.5 h (=0.6931/0.95=0.730 d). mu2=0.31/24=0.0129 h^-1; td=ln2/mu2=0.6931/0.0129=53.7 h (=2.24 d). Primary record uses the first-4-d exponential rate. No substrate uptake or product secretion rates in mmol/gDW/h are reported; substrate/N-utilization tests (glucose 5 mM +/- ammonium/nitrate 1 mM; Marine Basal Medium substrate tests with glycolate/DMSP/glutamate/glutamine/asparagine/aspartate at 12 mM carbon) report only growth yes/no, not rates. Medium is L1+Si ASW (cited from ref 78, Guillard/Hargraves L1); full component concentrations not given in-text, so composition left empty. pH not stated. Precultures: YTSS (4 g/L yeast extract, 2.5 g/L tryptone, 20 g/L sea salts) +/- 40 mM sucrose or glucose (1 mM)+ammonium (0.5 mM) in L1 ASW - these are inoculum/preparation media, not the growth-rate condition.

growth_records.medium: Defined filtered-seawater medium with propionate as sole carbon source

up.method: DOC/TOC drawdown (Shimadzu TOC-V analyzer) confirmed remineralization of the organic carbon substrate; no numeric specific uptake rate reported and no biomass in gDW given, so qS not derivable

growth_records.cite: Johnson, Winifred M et al. Evidence for quorum sensing and differential metabolite production by a marine bacterium in response to DMSP. The ISME Journal (2016). DOI:10.1038/ismej.2016.6. [PMC4989321]
growth_records.snippet: R. pomeroyi cells exhibited similar growth rates (0.1 h-1) on both substrates (DMSP or propionate), and remineralization of the organic carbon substrate was confirmed by the diminishing concentrations of DOC and TOC in the media
growth_records.notes: Experimental growth rate mu = 0.1 h-1 reported directly for R. pomeroyi DSS-3 on propionate. Doubling time computed: td = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.1 = 6.93 h. Carbon provided as 1 mM sodium propionate (= 3 mM carbon). No specific substrate-uptake or product-secretion rates in mmol/gDW/h were reported; the paper only qualitatively confirms carbon remineralization via DOC/TOC drawdown, and biomass was measured as cells/mL (10^6-10^7), not gDW, so qS cannot be derived. No pH stated. This is a metabolomics paper (targeted + untargeted MS) contrasting DMSP vs propionate growth; the only quantitative physiological rate is mu.

growth_records.medium: Defined filtered-seawater medium with DMSP as sole carbon source

up.method: DOC/TOC drawdown confirmed remineralization; DMSP supplied at 0.6 mM (= 3 mM carbon). No numeric specific uptake rate reported; biomass in cells/mL not gDW, so qS not derivable

sec.method: gas chromatography (adapted from Levine et al. 2012); detected to confirm the DMSP cleavage pathway was active but explicitly not quantified
gas chromatography; detected to confirm the DMSP demethylation pathway was active but explicitly not quantified

growth_records.cite: Johnson, Winifred M et al. Evidence for quorum sensing and differential metabolite production by a marine bacterium in response to DMSP. The ISME Journal (2016). DOI:10.1038/ismej.2016.6. [PMC4989321]
growth_records.snippet: R. pomeroyi cells exhibited similar growth rates (0.1 h-1) on both substrates (DMSP or propionate)... Methanethiol and dimethylsulfide (DMS), which are products of two different DMSP degradation pathways, were produced during growth on DMSP
growth_records.notes: Experimental growth rate mu = 0.1 h-1 reported directly for R. pomeroyi DSS-3 on DMSP (same rate as on propionate). Doubling time computed: td = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.1 = 6.93 h. Carbon supplied as 0.6 mM DMSP (= 3 mM carbon, matched to the propionate treatment). Secreted DMS and methanethiol were detected by GC (confirming both DMSP catabolic pathways) but explicitly not quantified, so no numeric secretion rate is available. No specific uptake rate reported; biomass was cells/mL (10^6-10^7), not gDW, so qS not derivable. No pH stated.

growth_records.medium: Marine ammonium mineral salts (MAMS) medium (modified after Schafer) + sole carbon source

sec.method: NH4+ accumulated in medium when choline/GBT used as sole C+N source (qualitative, Fig 3); no rate given

growth_records.cite: Lidbury I et al. Comparative genomics and mutagenesis analyses of choline metabolism in the marine Roseobacter clade. Environmental Microbiology (2015). DOI:10.1111/1462-2920.12943. [PMC4744692]
growth_records.snippet: Wild-type R. pomeroyi grew well on choline (mu = 0.073 +/- 0.003 h-1) and GBT (mu = 0.0860 +/- 0.001 h-1) ... Growth on COS was slightly slower (mu = 0.043 +/- 0.000 h-1).
growth_records.notes: mu = 0.073 +/- 0.003 h-1 reported directly on choline. doubling_time = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.073 = 9.49 h. MAMS medium composition cited from Schafer (not reproduced in paper) => medium_composition_stated:false. Bulk substrate depletion: 5 mM choline depleted from medium in 95 h => 5/95 = 0.0526 mM/h (volumetric, not biomass-specific; qS in mmol/gDW/h not computable - biomass not reported). Ammonium remineralized when substrate is sole C+N source (Fig 3), suppressed by adding 10 mM glucose (N-limiting). Temperature/pH not stated in text.

Ammonium remineralized when substrate is sole C+N source (Fig 3), suppressed by adding 10 mM glucose (N-limiting). Temperature/pH not stated in text.

phenotypes.base_medium: Sole-substrate growth / assimilation on a defined minimal medium (substrate as C/N/energy source) — positive = the organism grows on it
